---
title: "Quarto 4 — Equations, Cross-Refs, Citations"
subtitle: "The grammar of scientific writing"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goals — 45 minutes

::: {.incremental}
- LaTeX equations (inline, display, NIEHS examples)
- Cross-references for figs, tables, equations, sections
- BibTeX + CSL citation styles
- Same syntax as R Markdown; Quarto improves cross-refs
:::

# Equations {.section-divider}

## Inline vs display

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Inline**

`$\alpha = 0.05$`

renders as $\alpha = 0.05$
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Display**

`$$Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X$$`

$$Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X$$
:::
::::

## NIEHS examples

Hill model:

$$\text{Response} = \frac{E_{\max} \cdot C^n}{EC_{50}^n + C^n}$$

::: {.fragment}
Hazard quotient:

$$\text{HQ} = \frac{\text{CDI}}{\text{RfD}}$$
:::

## LADD

$$\text{LADD} = \frac{C \times IR \times EF \times ED}{BW \times AT}$$

::: {.fragment}
Where $C$ is concentration, $IR$ intake rate, $EF$ exposure frequency, $ED$ exposure duration, $BW$ body weight, $AT$ averaging time.
:::

## Equation cross-references

```markdown
$$
Y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_i + \varepsilon_i
$$ {#eq-linear}
```

::: {.incremental}
- Label goes **right after `$$`**, on the same line or next
- Reference with `@eq-linear` → "Equation 1"
- Built into Quarto (R Markdown needs `bookdown`)
:::

# Cross-references {.section-divider}

## Two requirements

::: {.incremental}
1. **Label with the right prefix**
2. **A caption** (figs and tables)
:::

| Prefix | What it labels |
|--------|----------------|
| `fig-` | Figures |
| `tbl-` | Tables |
| `eq-` | Equations |
| `sec-` | Sections (need `number-sections: true`) |
| `lst-` | Code listings |

## The R Markdown gap

| Feature | R Markdown | Quarto |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Figure cross-refs | Need `bookdown` | Built in |
| Table cross-refs | Need `bookdown` | Built in |
| Equation cross-refs | `bookdown` + `(\#eq:foo)` | `{#eq-foo}` |
| Section cross-refs | Manual anchors | `@sec-name` |

::: {.fragment}
Same end result; less plumbing.
:::

# Citations {.section-divider}

## Setup

```yaml
---
bibliography: references.bib
csl: nature.csl
---
```

Or load CSL by URL:

```yaml
csl: https://www.zotero.org/styles/jama
```

## Citing in prose

| You write | You get |
|-----------|--------|
| `@nrc2009` | NRC (2009) |
| `[@nrc2009]` | (NRC 2009) |
| `[@nrc2009, p. 47]` | (NRC 2009, p. 47) |
| `[-@nrc2009]` | (2009) |
| `[@a; @b]` | (A; B) |

## Where bibliography entries come from

::: {.incremental}
- **Zotero** — collection → BibTeX
- **Google Scholar** — quote icon → BibTeX
- **PubMed** — `doi2bib.org`
- **Citation managers** — most export BibTeX
- **Or use quartobot** to skip the .bib altogether (optional bonus)
:::

# Format survival {.section-divider}

## What works where

| Feature | HTML | Word | PDF |
|---------|------|------|-----|
| Cross-refs | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Citations | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Equations | MathJax | Office math | LaTeX native |
| Callouts | styled | paragraph | framed |
| Tabsets | interactive | sequential | sequential |
| `DT::datatable` | interactive | screenshot | screenshot |

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 4

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/04_equations_crossrefs_citations.qmd`. Exercises 4.1, 4.2, 4.3.
:::

45 minutes. Then slides.
